title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins prospect James Hagens completes his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney hints at prospect Hagens turning pro soon, boosting the team for the season's final stretch.
Positioning at left wing seen as ideal for Hagens, possibly complementing Elias Lindholm and Morgan Geekie on Bruins' third line.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Is This Thing On?' featuring Bradley Cooper and Will Arnett is streaming on Disney+.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' starring Cillian Murphy is available on Netflix.
'Born to Bowl' narrated by Liev Schreiber is streaming on HBO Max.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora enters his eighth season managing the Red Sox, with a contract extending until the 2027 season.
Cora would not confirm if he plans to extend his contract beyond 2027, considering family priorities and team status.
Cora is 134 wins away from surpassing Terry Francona for second-most wins by a Red Sox manager.

title: The Bruins’ second line isn’t just a pleasant surprise. They’ve been one of the NHL’s best this year.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' second line of Viktor Arvidsson, Pavel Zacha, and Casey Mittelstadt has outscored opponents 35-16 this season.
Boston's second line has the second-best goal differential in the NHL, just behind the Colorado Avalanche's top line.
Pavel Zacha leads the line with 51 points, while Arvidsson and Mittelstadt contribute significantly to the Bruins' playoff push.

title: He’s running Boston to support kids and families at MGH
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Andrew Mahr, 37, from Needham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for MGH's pediatric cancer care and research.
Inspired by young patients, Mahr first ran in 2014, witnessing their courage at Heartbreak Hill during the race.
Mahr highlights the funding disparity for pediatric vs. adult cancer, emphasizing support for families at MGH.

title: ‘More money in my pocket is a no-brainer’: Readers weigh in on proposed income tax cut
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A proposed tax reduction in Massachusetts could save median households $1,250 a year but reduce state revenue by $5.1 billion annually.
Seventy-eight percent of over 200 Boston.com poll respondents support lowering the state income tax rate from 5% to 4%.
Governor Maura Healey opposes the tax cut, citing financial risks, while supporters argue it addresses state spending issues.

title: A voodoo doll, a love triangle, and an alleged poisoning: Testimony begins in Salisbury antifreeze murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judy Church stands trial for allegedly poisoning her boyfriend, Leroy Fowler, with antifreeze, amid claims of a tumultuous love triangle.
Prosecutors allege Church had joked about poisoning Fowler, and evidence includes a Powerade bottle with ethylene glycol and their DNA.
Defense argues Church's expressions were off-color jokes and there was insufficient evidence directly linking her to the poisoning.

title: Healey pushes to get rid of Sunday hunting ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor Maura Healey proposes legislation to lift Massachusetts' Sunday hunting ban and expand crossbow use for all hunters.
The proposal includes reducing required setback distances for bowhunters near dwellings from 500 feet to 250 feet.
Massachusetts and Maine are the last two states with a complete Sunday hunting ban, which Healey calls outdated.

title: Cape Cod magnet fisher pulls ‘pristine-looking’ derringer pistol from Bridgewater river
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Nate DeMontigny discovered a pristine derringer pistol encased and submerged in a Bridgewater river.
The find was shared in a YouTube video with over 1,700 views; the pistol looked new despite decades underwater.
DeMontigny contacted police immediately; the discovery gained popularity online, attracting 1.2 million views on Reddit.

title: Teen charged with murder after stabbing in Chelsea
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old man, Sergio Josue Castellanos, faces a murder charge after a fatal stabbing in Chelsea.
Chelsea police responded to a stabbing report at 6:45 p.m. Thursday near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue.
The victim, a 25-year-old man, died after surgery; other victims' details remain undisclosed as investigation continues.
